controls.get.description: Retrieves a reference to a Control object (Lua userdata). A control represents a fader, list, or other type of control. The reference number can be found in the control properties panel in the Preset Editor.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.desc: number, a preset object reference number (1 .. 1023).

controls.get.returns: userdata, an object representing the control.
controls.get.example: -- Retrieving a reference to given control

<control>:getId.description: Retrieves an identifier of the Control. The identifier is assigned to the Control in the preset JSON.
<control>:getId.returns: number, the reference of the control (1 .. 1023).
<control>:getId.example: -- Retrieving a control and getting its Id

<control>:setVisible.description: Changes the visibility of the given control. The initial visibility is defined in the Preset JSON. The setVisibility() function can update the visibility at runtime.

parameters.name: shouldBeVisible
parameters.desc: boolean, true when the Control will be visible. <control>:isVisible() Retrieves the visibility status of the control.

<control>:setVisible.returns: boolean, true when the control is currently visible.
<control>:setVisible.example: -- a function to toggle visibility of a control

<control>:isVisible.description: Retrieves the visibility status of the control.
<control>:isVisible.returns: boolean, true when the control is currently visible.
<control>:isVisible.example: -- a function to toggle visibility of a control

<control>:setName.description: Sets a new name of the control.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: string, the new name to assign to the control. <control>:getName() Retrieves the current name of the control.

<control>:setName.returns: string, the current name of the control.
<control>:setName.example: -- print out a name of given control

<control>:getName.description: Retrieves the current name of the control.
<control>:getName.returns: string, the current name of the control.
<control>:getName.example: -- print out a name of given control

<control>:setColor.description: Sets a new color for the control. Although 24-bit RGB 888 is used, the controller internally converts it to 16-bit RGB 565.

parameters.name: color
parameters.desc: number, a number representing the color as a 24-bit RGB value. <control>:getColor() Retrieves the current color of the control.

<control>:setColor.returns: number, a number representing the color as a 24-bit RGB value.
<control>:setColor.example:
-- A callback function that changes color of the control
-- when its value exceeds 100

<control>:getColor.description: Retrieves the current color of the control.
<control>:getColor.returns: number, a number representing the color as a 24-bit RGB value.
<control>:getColor.example:
-- A callback function that changes color of the control
-- when its value exceeds 100

<control>:setVariant.description: Sets a variant for the control. For a list of variants, see the Globals section below.

parameters.name: variant
parameters.desc: number, a number representing the variant. <control>:getVariant() Retrieves the current variant of the control.

parameters.name: x
parameters.desc: number, X position on the screen. y number, Y position on the screen. width number, width of the control. height number, height of the control. <control>:getBounds() Retrieves the bounding box (position and dimensions) of the control on the screen. Use the X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHTglobals to access individual members of the array.

<control>:setVariant.returns: array, an array consisting of x, y, width, height boundary box attributes.
<control>:setVariant.example: -- print out position and dimensions of given control

<control>:getVariant.description: Retrieves the current variant of the control.

parameters.name: x
parameters.desc: number, X position on the screen. y number, Y position on the screen. width number, width of the control. height number, height of the control. <control>:getBounds() Retrieves the bounding box (position and dimensions) of the control on the screen. Use the X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHTglobals to access individual members of the array.

<control>:getVariant.returns: array, an array consisting of x, y, width, height boundary box attributes.
<control>:getVariant.example: -- print out position and dimensions of given control

<control>:setBounds.description: Sets the bounding box (position and dimensions) of the control on the screen. The function expects an array to be passed as an argument. Use the X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHTglobals to access individual members of the array.

parameters.name: x
parameters.desc: number, X position on the screen. y number, Y position on the screen. width number, width of the control. height number, height of the control. <control>:getBounds() Retrieves the bounding box (position and dimensions) of the control on the screen. Use the X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHTglobals to access individual members of the array.

<control>:setBounds.returns: array, an array consisting of x, y, width, height boundary box attributes.
<control>:setBounds.example: -- print out position and dimensions of given control

<control>:getBounds.description: Retrieves the bounding box (position and dimensions) of the control on the screen. Use the X, Y, WIDTH, HEIGHTglobals to access individual members of the array.
<control>:getBounds.returns: array, an array consisting of x, y, width, height boundary box attributes.
<control>:getBounds.example: -- print out position and dimensions of given control

<control>:setPot.description: Assigns the control to a specified control set and pot. See the Globals section below for available Control Set and pot identifiers.

parameters.name: controlSet
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the Control Set to assign. pot number, a numeric identifier of the pot.

<control>:setPot.example: -- Reassign the control to different controlSet and pot

<control>:setSlot.description: Moves the given control to a preset slot on the current page. The Control Set and pot values are assigned automatically, and the control is made visible.

parameters.name: slot
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the page slot (1 .. 36). <control>:setSlot(slot, page) Moves the given control to a preset slot on a specified page. The Control Set and pot values are assigned automatically, and the control is made visible. This function is a variant of setSlot(slot), allowing you to specify the target page as well.

parameters.name: slot
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the page slot (1 .. 36). page number, a numeric identifier of the page (1 .. 12).

<control>:setSlot.example: -- Change location of the control within the 6x6 grid

<control>:getValueIds.description: Retrieves a list of all valueIds associated with the control. The valueIds are defined in the preset JSON. Retrieved valueIds, eg. value, attack can be used as parameters for the <control>:getValue(valueId) function.
<control>:getValueIds.returns: array, a list of value identifier strings.
<control>:getValueIds.example: -- list all value Ids of a control

<control>:getValue.description: Retrieves the Value_object of the given control using the valueId handle. The valueId is defined in the preset JSON. If not specified, value will be used as the default valueId. List of available valueIds: * value * attack * decay * slope * break * sustain * release * x * y * l1 * r1 * l2 * r2 * l3 * r3 * l4 * r4

parameters.name: valueId
parameters.desc: string, a text that identifies a specific value of a control.

<control>:getValue.returns: userdata, a reference to the Value object.
<control>:getValue.example: -- Display min and max display values

<control>:getValues.description: Retrieves a list of all Value_objects associated with the control. The value objects are defined in the JSON preset.
<control>:getValues.returns: array, a list of references to userdata Value objects.
<control>:getValues.example: -- list all value objects of a control

<control>:setPaintCallback.description: Assigns a function that defines how the control is drawn. This can only be used with Custom controls.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.desc: function, a reference to a Lua function. <control>:setTouchCallback(callback) Assigns a function that will be called when a display touch event is received for the given Custom control.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.desc: function, a reference to a Lua function. <control>:setPotCallback(callback) Assigns a function that will be called when a pot change event is received for the given Custom control.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.desc: function, a reference to a Lua function. <control>:repaint() Schedules a repaint of the control. The function is meant to be used inside the Custom control touch and pot change callbacks. <control>:print() Prints all attributes of the Control object to the Logger output. Try it yourself 🚀 Control_module_demo

<control>:setTouchCallback.description: Assigns a function that will be called when a display touch event is received for the given Custom control.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.desc: function, a reference to a Lua function. <control>:setPotCallback(callback) Assigns a function that will be called when a pot change event is received for the given Custom control.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.desc: function, a reference to a Lua function. <control>:repaint() Schedules a repaint of the control. The function is meant to be used inside the Custom control touch and pot change callbacks. <control>:print() Prints all attributes of the Control object to the Logger output. Try it yourself 🚀 Control_module_demo

<control>:setPotCallback.description: Assigns a function that will be called when a pot change event is received for the given Custom control.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.desc: function, a reference to a Lua function. <control>:repaint() Schedules a repaint of the control. The function is meant to be used inside the Custom control touch and pot change callbacks. <control>:print() Prints all attributes of the Control object to the Logger output. Try it yourself 🚀 Control_module_demo

<control>:repaint.description: Schedules a repaint of the control. The function is meant to be used inside the Custom control touch and pot change callbacks. <control>:print() Prints all attributes of the Control object to the Logger output. Try it yourself 🚀 Control_module_demo

pipe.acquire.description: Creates a new data pipe with a name. Other presets can listen to that pipe and receive any data sent by the preset that owns it.

parameters.name: pipeName
parameters.desc: string, name of the data pipe channel.

parameters.name: pipeId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the pipe to send the data to. value number, a floating-point number to send. pipe.release(pipeId) Releases a previously acquired pipe, making the pipeId available for other presets to use.

parameters.name: pipeId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the pipe to send the data to.

pipe.acquire.returns: number, a numeric identifier of the pipe channel. pipe.send(pipeId, value) Sends a floating-point number to a data pipe that was previously acquired using the `pipe.acquire()` function. The value will be delivered to all presets that are listening to that pipe.

pipe.send.description: Sends a floating-point number to a data pipe that was previously acquired using the `pipe.acquire()` function. The value will be delivered to all presets that are listening to that pipe.

parameters.name: pipeId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the pipe to send the data to. value number, a floating-point number to send. pipe.release(pipeId) Releases a previously acquired pipe, making the pipeId available for other presets to use.

parameters.name: pipeId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the pipe to send the data to.

pipe.release.description: Releases a previously acquired pipe, making the pipeId available for other presets to use.

parameters.name: pipeId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the pipe to send the data to.

devices.get.description: Retrieves a reference to a Device object (Lua userdata). A Device represents a MIDI device that sends and receives messages with the controller.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the device (1 .. 32).

parameters.name: port
parameters.desc: enum, a numeric identifier of the port [PORT_1, PORT_2] channel number, a numeric identifier of the MIDI channel (1 .. 16).

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of device to be created. name string, a name to be assigned to the device. port enum, a numeric identifier of the port [PORT_1, PORT_2] channel number, a numeric identifier of the MIDI channel (1 .. 16).

devices.get.returns: userdata, an object representing the device.

devices.getByPortChannel.description: Retrieves a reference to a Device object (Lua userdata) using the port and MIDI channel. A Device represents a MIDI device that sends and receives messages with the controller.

parameters.name: port
parameters.desc: enum, a numeric identifier of the port [PORT_1, PORT_2] channel number, a numeric identifier of the MIDI channel (1 .. 16).

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of device to be created. name string, a name to be assigned to the device. port enum, a numeric identifier of the port [PORT_1, PORT_2] channel number, a numeric identifier of the MIDI channel (1 .. 16).

devices.getByPortChannel.returns: userdata, an object representing the device.

devices.create.description: Creates a new Device object and returns a reference to it.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of device to be created. name string, a name to be assigned to the device. port enum, a numeric identifier of the port [PORT_1, PORT_2] channel number, a numeric identifier of the MIDI channel (1 .. 16).

devices.create.returns: userdata, an object representing the device.

<device>:getId.description: Retrieves an identifier of the Device. The identifier is assigned to the control in the preset JSON or created with the devices.create() function.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: string, a name to be assigned to the Device. <device>.getName() Retrieves the name currently assigned to the Device.

parameters.name: port
parameters.desc: enum, a numeric identifier of the port [PORT_1, PORT_2] <device>.getPort() Retrieves the currently assigned MIDI port.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the MIDI channel (1 .. 16). <device>.getChannel() Retrieves the currently assigned MIDI channel.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.desc: number, the time delay in msecs to be set. <device>.getRate() Retrieves the current time delay between sending individual MIDI messages.

<device>:getId.returns: number, the time delay in milliseconds.
<device>:getId.example:
-- This needs to reflect the preset device settings
local AccessVirusDeviceId = 2

events.subscribe.description: A function to instruct the controller what event notifications should be emitted. Subscribed events are result in calls to event callback functions and sending out SysEx event notifications.

parameters.name: eventFlags
parameters.desc: number, a byte where each bit represents an event type to be subscribed. Note, currently only `PAGES` and `POTS` are supported.

events.setPort.description: Sets MIDI port that will be used to send out event notifications.

parameters.name: port
parameters.desc: enum, a numeric representation of the MIDI port [PORT_1, PORT_2, PORT_CTRL]. events.onPageChange(newPageId, oldPageId) A callback function that runs automatically when the user switches to a different page. The function is called only if Page change notifications have been subscribed to. For more details, see Events section.

parameters.name: newPageId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the Page being activated (1–12). oldPageId number, a numeric identifier of the Page being left (1–12). events.onPotTouchChange(potId, controlId, touched) A callback function that is called when user touches or releases the controller knobs.

parameters.name: potId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the Pot being touched (1–12). controlId number, a preset object reference number (1 .. 1023). touched boolean, when true the Pot has an active touch, otherwise it was released.

events.onPageChange.description: A callback function that runs automatically when the user switches to a different page. The function is called only if Page change notifications have been subscribed to. For more details, see Events section.

parameters.name: newPageId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the Page being activated (1–12). oldPageId number, a numeric identifier of the Page being left (1–12). events.onPotTouchChange(potId, controlId, touched) A callback function that is called when user touches or releases the controller knobs.

parameters.name: potId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the Pot being touched (1–12). controlId number, a preset object reference number (1 .. 1023). touched boolean, when true the Pot has an active touch, otherwise it was released.

events.onPotTouchChange.description: A callback function that is called when user touches or releases the controller knobs.

parameters.name: potId
parameters.desc: number, a numeric identifier of the Pot being touched (1–12). controlId number, a preset object reference number (1 .. 1023). touched boolean, when true the Pot has an active touch, otherwise it was released.

parameters.name: color
parameters.desc: a number representing the color as a 24-bit RGB value. graphics.drawPixel(x1, y1) Draws single pixel at (x, y) coordinates.

parameters.name: x
parameters.desc: number, X position of the starting point of the line. y number, Y position on the starting point of the line. graphics.drawLine(x1, y1, x2, y2) Draws a straight line between (x1, y2) staring coordinates and (x2, y2) ending coordinates.

parameters.name: x1

parameters.name: x
parameters.desc: number, X position of the left-top corner of the rectangle. y number, Y position on the left-top corner of the rectangle. width number, width of the rectangle. height number, height of the rectangle. graphics.fillRect(x, y, width, height) Draws a solid-filled rectangle at the (x, y) coordinates with the given width and height.

parameters.name: x

midi.onMessage.description: A user-defined callback function to handle all types of MIDI messages.

overlays.get.description: Retrieves a reference to an Overlay object (Lua userdata). The overlay id number can be found in the control properties panel in the Preset Editor.

parameters.name: overlayId
parameters.desc: number, an overlay identifier.

parameters.name: overlayId
parameters.desc: number, an identifier of the overlay to be created. overlayData table, a Lua table with the overlay items data.

overlays.get.returns: userdata, an object representing the overlay. The Overlay Lua table must be structured as shown below. value is the MIDI value, label is a text label associated with the MIDI value. lua overlayData = { { value = 1, label = "Room" }, { value = 2, label = "Hall" }, { value = 3, label = "Plate" }, { value = 4, label = "Spring" } }

overlays.create.description: Creates a new Overlay object and returns a reference (userdata) to it. The overlay ID must be unique; otherwise, Electra will log a conflict in the debug log and overwrite the existing overlay.

parameters.name: overlayId
parameters.desc: number, an identifier of the overlay to be created. overlayData table, a Lua table with the overlay items data.

overlays.create.returns: userdata, an object representing the overlay. The Overlay Lua table must be structured as shown below. value is the MIDI value, label is a text label associated with the MIDI value. lua overlayData = { { value = 1, label = "Room" }, { value = 2, label = "Hall" }, { value = 3, label = "Plate" }, { value = 4, label = "Spring" } }

<overlay>:print.description: Prints all attributes of the Overlay object to the Logger output.

parameterMap.resetAll.description: Removes all entries from the Parameter Map, leaving it completely empty. parameterMap.resetDevice(deviceId) Resets all parameters of the given device to their initial (unset) state. Important: 'Unset' does not mean 0 or a default value; it means the value is empty and undefined.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, an identifier of the device to reset (1 .. 32). parameterMap.set(deviceId, type, parameterNumber, midiValue) Updates the MIDI value of a specific Parameter Map entry. This will automatically send the MIDI messages and call any related Lua function callbacks.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, an identifier of the device to reset (1 .. 32). type number, an identifier of the Message parameter type (0 .. 16). parameterNumber number, a numeric identifier of the Message parameter (0 .. 16383). midiValue number, the MIDI value to set and send (0 .. 16383). parameterMap.apply(deviceId, type, parameterNumber, midiValue) Updates a specific Parameter Map entry by applying a MIDI value fragment. It uses a logical OR between the current value and the new fragment. Afterward, the system automatically sends the MIDI messages and calls any related Lua function callbacks.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, an identifier of the device to reset (1 .. 32). type number, an identifier of the Message parameter type (0 .. 16). parameterNumber number, a numeric identifier of the Message parameter (0 .. 16383). midiValueFragment number, the MIDI value to apply (0 .. 16383). parameterMap.modulate(deviceId, type, parameterNumber, modulationValue, depth) Temporarily changes (modulates) the MIDI value of a Parameter Map entry. The modulated value is sent, but it is not saved in the Parameter Map or processed by Lua callbacks or value formatters.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, an identifier of the device to reset (1 .. 32). type number, an identifier of the Message parameter type (0 .. 16). parameterNumber number, a numeric identifier of the Message parameter (0 .. 16383). modulationValue float, a modulation signal to be applied (-1.0 .. 1.0). depth number, a depth of the modulation signal (0 .. 100). parameterMap.updateValue(deviceId, type, parameterNumber, midiValue) Updates the MIDI value of a specific Parameter Map entry. Unlike the set() function, no MIDI messages are sent and no Lua callbacks are invoked.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, an identifier of the device to reset (1 .. 32). type number, an identifier of the Message parameter type (0 .. 16). parameterNumber number, a numeric identifier of the Message parameter (0 .. 16383). midiValue number, the MIDI value to set and send (0 .. 16383). parameterMap.get(deviceId, type, parameterNumber) Retrieves the current MIDI value stored in the Parameter Map entry. This function is equivalent to calling message:getValue() on a Message object with the corresponding attributes.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, an identifier of the device to reset (1 .. 32). type number, an identifier of the Message parameter type (0 .. 16). parameterNumber number, a numeric identifier of the Message parameter (0 .. 16383). parameterMap.getValues(deviceId, type, parameterNumber) Retrieves a list of all Value objects linked to the Parameter Map entry. These are the Value objects that will be updated when the MIDI value changes.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.desc: number, an identifier of the device to reset (1 .. 32). type number, an identifier of the Message parameter type (0 .. 16). parameterNumber number, a numeric identifier of the Message parameter (0 .. 16383). parameterMap.onChange(valueObjects, origin, midiValue) Retrieves a list of all Value objects linked to the Parameter Map entry. These are the Value objects that will be updated when the MIDI value changes.

parameters.name: valueObjects
parameters.desc: array, a list of references to userdata Value objects associated with the ParameterMap entry. origin number, a numeric identifier of the change origin (see [Globals](./ luaext.html#globals) for details). midiValue number, the current MIDI value of the ParameterMap entry (0 .. 16383).

preset.onLoad.description: A callback function that is called immediately after the preset is loaded, but before the default values are initialized. preset.onReady() A callback function that is called when the preset is fully initialized and ready to be used. A callback function that is called when user actives the preset by changing currently selected preset slot. preset.onLeave() A callback function that is called when user leaves the preset in order to switch to another preset slot. preset.onExit() A callback function that is called when preset is removed from the controller. Note, it is not called when user switches to another preset.

window.repaint.description: Forces repaint of the current window. window.stop() Stops automatic repainting of components. This helps when updating many controls at once. By using window.stop() before and window.resume() after your updates, you can speed up the process and display all changes together. window.resume() Resumes the process of automatic repainting of components. The complete repaint of whole window is forced at the moment when the repainting process is resumed.
